entry.title: Azure Batch
entry.description: Azure Batch is a managed service for running large-scale parallel and high-performance computing (HPC) batch jobs in the cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes limited compute resources for batch jobs.
limits: 750 hours of low-priority VMs per month
caveats: Low-priority VMs may be preempted
Storage and networking billed separately
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if you exceed free VM hours or use premium resources

entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Batch for automating and scaling compute-intensive batch workloads that can run in parallel across many nodes.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need low-latency, interactive workloads or require guaranteed VM availability at all times.
entry.body: Azure Batch enables users to efficiently run large-scale parallel and high-performance computing workloads in the cloud, automating resource management and job scheduling.

entry.title: Azure Container Apps
entry.description: Azure Container Apps is a managed service for running containerized applications and microservices without managing infrastructure.

freeTierDetails.summary: Includes a free monthly grant of vCPU and memory resources for containerized workloads.
caveats: Overages billed at standard rates
Networking and storage may incur additional costs
billingRiskNotes: Resource usage beyond free tier is automatically billed
Monitor usage to avoid unexpected charges

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to deploy containerized applications without managing Kubernetes or underlying infrastructure, especially for scalable microservices.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require full Kubernetes control, persistent storage, or need to run stateful workloads with complex networking.
entry.body: Azure Container Apps provides a serverless platform for deploying and scaling containerized applications, ideal for microservices and event-driven workloads without infrastructure management.

entry.title: Azure Container Registry
entry.description: Azure Container Registry is a managed Docker container registry for storing and managing container images on Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: The Basic tier offers a free usage quota with limited storage and image operations.
limits: Unlimited image pulls/pushes within storage limit
caveats: Storage and throughput are limited on the free tier
No geo-replication
No SLA for Basic tier
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding storage incurs charges
Upgrading to higher tiers increases costs

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed private container registry tightly integrated with Azure services for development or small-scale production workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need geo-replication, high throughput, or enterprise-grade SLAs, or if you require a fully free solution without a credit card.
avoidIf: You require high storage or throughput
You want a no-credit-card free tier
entry.body: Azure Container Registry provides a simple, managed way to store and manage container images, with seamless integration into Azure's ecosystem and CI/CD workflows.

entry.title: Azure Functions
entry.description: Azure Functions is a serverless compute service for running event-driven code without managing infrastructure.

freeTierDetails.summary: 1 million free requests and 400,000 GB-s compute per month.
caveats: Consumption plan only
Premium and Dedicated plans not included
Networking features may incur charges
billingRiskNotes: Overages billed automatically if limits exceeded
Premium features not included in free tier

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to run code in response to events or HTTP requests without managing servers, especially for variable or unpredictable workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, predictable low-latency, or require advanced networking or VNET integration on the free tier.
entry.body: Azure Functions enables you to build and deploy event-driven applications without managing infrastructure, with a generous free tier for development and light production use. It is ideal for APIs, automation, and event processing, but has limitations for long-running or network-intensive workloads on the free tier.

entry.title: Azure Kubernetes Service
entry.description: Managed Kubernetes clusters on Azure with integrated scaling, monitoring, and security features.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes no charge for AKS control plane; you pay only for agent nodes and additional resources.
limits: No charge for AKS control plane
Pay for VM nodes and storage
Standard networking and monitoring may incur costs
caveats: Agent nodes, storage, and outbound data transfer are billed separately
Some features (e.g., advanced networking) are not free
billingRiskNotes: Costs can increase quickly with node scaling or extra services

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need managed Kubernetes clusters for scalable, production workloads with Azure integration and minimal operational overhead.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need a fully free solution, have minimal container workloads, or require deep customization of the Kubernetes control plane.
avoidIf: You want full control over Kubernetes internals
entry.body: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) provides a managed Kubernetes environment, automating cluster management, scaling, and integration with Azure services. The control plane is free, but you pay for agent nodes and additional resources. AKS is ideal for teams seeking to deploy, scale, and manage containerized applications in production with minimal infrastructure management. Be aware that costs can rise with node scaling, premium features, or additional Azure services.

entry.title: Azure VM Image Builder
entry.description: Azure VM Image Builder automates creation, customization, and management of VM images at scale in Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 20 free custom image builds per month.
caveats: Standard Azure VM and storage costs apply for resources used during image creation.
Overages billed per additional image build.
billingRiskNotes: Resource usage during builds may incur additional charges.
Exceeding free builds results in per-build charges.

useCases: Integrating image builds into CI/CD pipelines
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to automate and standardize VM image creation for consistent deployments across Azure environments.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require image building for non-Azure clouds or need a fully free solution without associated resource costs.
entry.body: Azure VM Image Builder enables automated creation and customization of VM images, supporting integration with CI/CD pipelines and ensuring consistent deployments across Azure environments. The free tier allows up to 20 custom image builds per month, but users should be aware that standard Azure VM and storage costs apply during the image creation process. This service is best suited for teams and enterprises looking to standardize and automate VM deployments within Azure, but may not be ideal for those with strict cost constraints or multi-cloud requirements.

entry.title: Cloud Functions
entry.description: Cloud Functions is a serverless compute service for running event-driven code without managing servers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Generous always-free tier for invocations, compute time, and networking.
caveats: Limits apply per billing account, not per project.
Overages billed automatically.
Some regions may have different limits.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free limits incurs charges automatically.
No hard cap; monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs.

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to run code in response to events without managing servers or infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, high memory/CPU, or predictable low-latency performance.
entry.body: Cloud Functions lets you deploy single-purpose functions that respond to HTTP requests or cloud events, scaling automatically with demand and requiring no server management.

entry.title: Cloud Run
entry.description: Cloud Run is a fully managed compute platform for running containerized applications and APIs on demand.

freeTierDetails.summary: Cloud Run offers a generous always-free tier for containerized HTTP workloads.
caveats: Only for one GCP billing account per month
Outbound networking and additional resources may incur charges
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free limits incurs automatic charges.
Some features (e.g., custom domains, VPC) may not be free.

entry.whenToUse: Use Cloud Run for quick deployment of stateless containerized apps or APIs without managing infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need persistent storage, long-running background processes, or require fine-grained VM control.
entry.body: Cloud Run lets you deploy and scale containerized applications with minimal configuration, charging only for actual usage and offering a robust free tier for low-traffic workloads.

entry.title: Cloudflare Workers
entry.description: Cloudflare Workers is a serverless platform for deploying JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly code at the edge.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers 100,000 requests per day with limited CPU time and features.
limits: No custom domains on free plan
caveats: KV, Durable Objects, and other add-ons have separate limits
No guaranteed uptime or SLA on free tier

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need fast, globally distributed serverless functions for lightweight APIs, request handling, or edge logic with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, heavy computation, or require advanced networking features not supported by the platform.
entry.body: Cloudflare Workers enables developers to deploy serverless code at the edge, offering fast response times and global distribution with a generous free tier.

entry.title: Cloudflare Workers KV
entry.description: A globally distributed key-value storage service for low-latency reads and simple data storage with Cloudflare Workers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1 GB storage, 100,000 read operations, and 1,000 write/delete operations per day.
caveats: Eventual consistency
No strong consistency guarantees
Limited query capabilities
billingRiskNotes: Hard caps prevent unexpected charges

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need simple, low-latency key-value storage at the edge for small data and high-read workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require strong consistency, complex queries, or transactional guarantees for your data.
entry.body: Cloudflare Workers KV is a globally distributed key-value store designed for low-latency access and simple data storage, ideal for edge applications needing fast reads and basic persistence.

entry.title: Compute Engine e2-micro
entry.description: Google Cloud's always-free e2-micro VM offers basic compute resources for lightweight workloads with limited performance requirements.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always-free usage of one e2-micro VM instance per month in select regions.
caveats: Performance is limited and may burst only briefly
Not suitable for production workloads
Linux and Windows supported
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if you exceed free tier limits or use paid features

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a free, persistent VM for development, learning, or very low-traffic apps with minimal resource needs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high performance, scalability, or need to run production workloads with strict uptime or resource guarantees.
entry.body: Compute Engine e2-micro provides a free, always-on virtual machine suitable for learning, prototyping, and low-traffic applications, with strict resource and region limitations.

entry.title: EMQX Serverless
entry.description: EMQX Serverless provides a managed, scalable MQTT broker for real-time messaging without infrastructure management.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes basic MQTT messaging with limited connections and traffic.
caveats: Overages require paid plan
Limited to one serverless deployment

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed MQTT broker for prototyping, small-scale IoT, or real-time messaging without handling infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced customizations, high throughput, or on-premises deployment beyond free tier limits.
entry.body: EMQX Serverless offers a managed MQTT broker with a free tier suitable for prototyping and small-scale IoT or messaging workloads, removing infrastructure overhead.

entry.title: Firebase Cloud Functions
entry.description: Firebase Cloud Functions lets you run backend code in response to events triggered by Firebase features and HTTPS requests.

freeTierDetails.summary: Generous always-free tier for invocations, compute time, and outbound networking.
caveats: Outbound networking beyond 5GB/month incurs charges
Cold starts may affect latency
Limited to Firebase-supported regions
billingRiskNotes: Overages are billed automatically if limits are exceeded

useCases: Trigger backend logic on Firestore/Realtime Database changes
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to run backend code in response to Firebase events or simple HTTPS requests without managing servers.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for heavy compute workloads, low-latency APIs, or when you need full control over runtime environment or regional placement.
entry.body: Firebase Cloud Functions enables developers to deploy serverless backend logic that responds to Firebase events or HTTP requests, with a generous free tier suitable for prototyping and small-scale production. It is ideal for automating workflows, integrating with Firebase services, and building lightweight APIs, but may not suit heavy compute or latency-sensitive applications.

entry.title: Google Kubernetes Engine
entry.description: Managed Kubernetes service for deploying, managing, and scaling containerized applications on Google Cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: One zonal cluster per month free, plus free control plane management.
limits: 1 Autopilot or Standard zonal cluster per billing account
No cluster management fee for first cluster
Node and resource usage billed separately
caveats: Compute, storage, and network resources are billed separately
Free tier applies only to cluster management fees
billingRiskNotes: Resource usage beyond free cluster incurs charges
Nodes, storage, and network are not included in free tier

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need managed Kubernetes for scalable container workloads and want to avoid cluster management overhead.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require fully free compute resources or have minimal Kubernetes experience and need a simpler deployment option.
entry.body: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) offers a fully managed Kubernetes platform for deploying, managing, and scaling containerized applications, with a free tier covering cluster management fees for one zonal cluster per month. While GKE simplifies Kubernetes operations and provides robust scalability, users are responsible for compute, storage, and network costs, and should be familiar with Kubernetes concepts for effective use.

entry.title: Khan Academy
entry.description: Khan Academy offers free interactive courses and exercises in computer programming, math, science, and more for learners of all ages.

freeTierDetails.summary: All courses and exercises are available for free with no payment required.
caveats: No official certifications
Primarily focused on foundational topics

entry.whenToUse: Use Khan Academy for self-paced learning or to build foundational skills in programming, math, or science without cost barriers.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced, specialized, or accredited courses, or need official certifications for professional purposes.
entry.body: Khan Academy provides a wide range of free, interactive courses and exercises in programming, math, science, and other subjects, making it ideal for beginners and students seeking foundational knowledge. The platform is best suited for self-paced learning and practice, but does not offer official certifications or advanced professional training.

entry.title: Lambda
entry.description: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that runs code in response to events without provisioning or managing servers.

freeTierDetails.summary: 1M free requests and 400,000 GB-seconds compute time per month.
caveats: Limits apply per account per month
Charges apply for additional usage
Some integrations may incur extra costs
billingRiskNotes: Overages billed automatically if limits exceeded

entry.whenToUse: Use Lambda for event-driven workloads, lightweight APIs, or automation where you want to avoid server management and scale automatically.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Lambda for long-running processes, high-throughput workloads with low latency needs, or when you need fine-grained control over infrastructure.
entry.body: AWS Lambda lets you run code in response to triggers without managing servers, making it ideal for event-driven and microservice architectures.

entry.title: Oracle Always Free Compute
entry.description: Oracle Always Free Compute offers limited, no-cost virtual machines suitable for basic workloads and development.

freeTierDetails.summary: Run up to 4 ARM or 2 AMD VMs with limited resources at no cost.
limits: 1 GB RAM per AMD VM, 24 GB RAM total for ARM
caveats: Limited to specific shapes and regions
No GPU or advanced networking
Strict resource quotas
billingRiskNotes: No charges unless you exceed free tier limits or provision paid resources

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need persistent, low-cost VMs for development, testing, or small-scale hosting without incurring charges.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high performance, large-scale deployments, GPU access, or advanced networking features.
entry.body: Oracle Always Free Compute provides a persistent, no-cost VM environment suitable for learning, prototyping, and small personal or open source projects. It is ideal for users who want to experiment with cloud infrastructure or host lightweight services without incurring costs, but is not suitable for production workloads requiring high performance, advanced features, or enterprise support.

entry.title: Supabase Edge Functions
entry.description: Supabase Edge Functions let you deploy serverless TypeScript functions close to your users for low-latency APIs and backend logic.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes limited monthly invocations and execution time for Edge Functions.
caveats: Limits shared with other Supabase free tier resources
No custom domains on free tier

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need lightweight, serverless backend logic or APIs deployed close to users with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, heavy computation, or require custom networking beyond HTTP APIs.
entry.body: Supabase Edge Functions provide a simple way to deploy serverless TypeScript functions at the edge, ideal for APIs, webhooks, and lightweight backend logic with generous free tier limits.

entry.title: Virtual Machines
entry.description: Provision scalable, on-demand virtual servers for running applications, development, or testing workloads in the cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: 750 hours per month of select B1S Linux or Windows VM for 12 months.
caveats: Only specific VM sizes eligible
Storage and bandwidth limits apply
Requires Azure account
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue if usage exceeds limits or after 12 months

entry.whenToUse: Use when you need flexible, isolated compute resources for development, testing, or learning cloud infrastructure with minimal upfront cost.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need always-free, long-term compute or require high-performance, production-grade workloads without incurring costs.
entry.body: Azure Virtual Machines provide on-demand, scalable compute resources for a variety of workloads, with a limited free tier suitable for development and learning.

entry.title: Azure Batch
entry.description: Azure Batch enables large-scale parallel and high-performance computing (HPC) workloads in the cloud with job scheduling and resource management.

freeTierDetails.summary: Limited free usage of Batch compute and management; only compute resources used are billed.
limits: No charge for Batch management and job scheduling
Free tier applies to management, not compute
caveats: You pay for underlying compute, storage, and networking
No dedicated always-free compute hours
billingRiskNotes: Compute usage can incur significant costs if not monitored

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and create a Batch account.
Create a pool of compute nodes (VMs) in your Batch account.
Define and upload your job and tasks to the Batch service.
Monitor job progress and retrieve output from storage.
Shut down or resize pools to control costs.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Batch for automating large-scale parallel or compute-intensive workloads that can be split into independent tasks.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need always-free compute or have small, infrequent jobs better suited for serverless or managed workflow services.
avoidIf: Your workloads are small or event-driven
entry.body: Azure Batch provides job scheduling and resource management for large-scale parallel and high-performance computing workloads, charging only for the compute resources used.

entry.title: Azure Container Apps
entry.description: Azure Container Apps enables you to run microservices and containerized applications without managing infrastructure.

freeTierDetails.summary: Includes 180,000 vCPU-seconds, 360,000 GiB-seconds, and 2 million requests per month free.
caveats: Only applies to consumption plan usage
Networking and storage billed separately
billingRiskNotes: Overages billed automatically if limits exceeded

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and create a new Container Apps environment.
Deploy your container image from a registry (e.g., Docker Hub or Azure Container Registry).
Configure scaling rules and environment variables as needed.
Set up ingress to expose your app to the internet.
Monitor usage and logs via Azure Portal.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to deploy containerized apps or microservices without managing Kubernetes or infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require fine-grained control over orchestration, custom networking, or persistent storage beyond basic options.
entry.body: Azure Container Apps provides a serverless platform for running containers and microservices, offering automatic scaling and integrated monitoring without managing Kubernetes.

entry.title: Azure Container Registry
entry.description: Azure Container Registry is a managed Docker container registry for storing and managing private container images in Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier (Basic SKU) allows limited storage and image operations per month.
limits: Unlimited image pulls and pushes (subject to bandwidth limits)
caveats: No geo-replication
Limited performance compared to higher SKUs
billingRiskNotes: Storage overages and premium features incur charges

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new Container Registry using the Basic SKU.
Configure access credentials or enable Azure Active Directory authentication.
Push a Docker image to your registry using the Azure CLI.
Pull the image from your registry to your deployment environment.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed private container registry tightly integrated with Azure services and workflows.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require geo-replication, high throughput, or plan to exceed the free storage and webhook limits.
entry.body: Azure Container Registry provides a secure, managed Docker registry service for storing and managing container images, with seamless Azure integration and a limited free tier.

entry.title: Azure Functions
entry.description: Azure Functions is a serverless compute service for running event-driven code without managing infrastructure.

freeTierDetails.summary: 1 million free requests and 400,000 GB-s compute per month.
caveats: Consumption plan only
Premium and Dedicated plans not included
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if usage exceeds free tier limits

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new Function App resource.
Choose the Consumption plan for always-free tier.
Select your preferred runtime (e.g., Node.js, Python, C#).
Deploy your first function using the portal or CLI.
entry.whenToUse: Use Azure Functions for lightweight, event-driven workloads where you want to avoid server management and pay only for actual usage.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, require dedicated compute, or have strict latency requirements not suited to cold starts.
entry.body: Azure Functions enables you to build scalable, event-driven applications with minimal setup, ideal for APIs, automation, and background processing on a pay-per-use model.

entry.title: Azure Kubernetes Service
entry.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) provides managed Kubernetes clusters for deploying, scaling, and managing containerized applications on Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: AKS offers free managed Kubernetes control plane with paid node VMs.
limits: Pay only for VM nodes and storage
Standard networking and load balancer charges apply
caveats: Node VMs, storage, and outbound data transfer are billed separately
Free tier does not include node costs
billingRiskNotes: Costs can accrue quickly from node VMs and network usage

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and create a new resource group.
Navigate to Azure Kubernetes Service and start a new cluster deployment.
Configure cluster basics (region, node size, node count).
Review and create the AKS cluster.
Install and configure kubectl to connect to your AKS cluster.
entry.whenToUse: Use AKS when you need a managed Kubernetes environment for deploying and scaling containerized workloads on Azure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid AKS if you want a fully free solution or have minimal container orchestration needs; node and network costs can add up.
entry.body: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes platform that simplifies deploying, managing, and scaling containerized applications in the Azure cloud.

entry.title: Azure VM Image Builder
entry.description: Azure VM Image Builder automates the creation, customization, and management of virtual machine images at scale.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 20 custom image builds per month.
limits: Standard VM and storage charges apply
caveats: VM and storage usage during builds may incur additional costs
Free tier may not cover large-scale or frequent builds
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply for VM and storage resources used during image creation

useCases: Integrating image builds into CI/CD pipelines
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and ensure you have a subscription.
Register the Image Builder resource provider in your subscription.
Create an Image Template using Azure CLI or ARM template.
Configure source image, customizations, and destination settings.
Start the image build process and monitor progress in the portal.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to automate and standardize VM image creation for Azure deployments.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require image building for non-Azure platforms or need unlimited builds without incurring extra costs.
entry.body: Azure VM Image Builder helps automate the creation and management of custom VM images, supporting standardized environments and integration with CI/CD workflows.

entry.title: Cloud Functions
entry.description: Google Cloud Functions is a serverless compute service for running event-driven code without managing servers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Generous always-free tier for lightweight workloads.
caveats: Limits apply per billing account, not per project.
Outbound networking beyond 5GB is billed.
Some regions may have different quotas.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free tier incurs charges automatically.
Monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs.

quickstartSteps: Create a Google Cloud account and enable billing.
Enable the Cloud Functions API in your project.
Install and initialize the Google Cloud SDK.
Write your function code and define a trigger.
Deploy the function using the gcloud CLI.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to run code in response to events without managing servers or infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for long-running tasks, high-throughput compute, or when you need fine-grained control over runtime environments.
entry.body: Google Cloud Functions lets you deploy and run code in response to HTTP requests or cloud events, with a generous free tier for small workloads.

entry.title: Cloud Run
entry.description: Cloud Run is a fully managed serverless platform by Google Cloud for running containerized applications and APIs.

freeTierDetails.summary: Cloud Run offers a generous always-free tier for containerized workloads.
caveats: Free tier applies per billing account, not per project.
Outbound traffic beyond 1 GiB/month is billed.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free tier limits incurs charges.
No hard cap; monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Google Cloud account and enable billing.
Enable the Cloud Run API in your project.
Build and push your container image to Google Container Registry or Artifact Registry.
Deploy your container to Cloud Run using the Google Cloud Console or gcloud CLI.
Configure authentication and set environment variables as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Cloud Run when you want to deploy containerized applications without managing servers or infrastructure, and need automatic scaling.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Cloud Run for stateful workloads, applications requiring persistent local storage, or when you need fine-grained control over infrastructure.
entry.body: Cloud Run lets you deploy and scale containerized applications easily, charging only for actual usage, with a generous always-free tier for small workloads.

entry.title: Cloudflare Workers
entry.description: Cloudflare Workers lets you deploy serverless code globally at the edge for fast, scalable web applications and APIs.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 100,000 requests per day with no credit card required.
caveats: KV storage and other add-ons have separate limits.
CPU time is capped per request.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Cloudflare account.
Install the Wrangler CLI tool.
Run 'wrangler init' to create a new project.
Develop your Worker using JavaScript or TypeScript.
Deploy with 'wrangler publish'.
entry.whenToUse: Use Cloudflare Workers to deploy lightweight APIs or logic close to users for low-latency, globally distributed applications.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, large memory, or advanced language/runtime support beyond JavaScript/TypeScript.
entry.body: Cloudflare Workers enables developers to run serverless code at the edge, providing fast response times and global scalability for APIs and web applications. The free tier is generous for small projects, but resource limits and language/runtime constraints apply.

entry.title: Cloudflare Workers KV
entry.description: Cloudflare Workers KV is a globally distributed key-value storage for low-latency edge applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 100,000 read operations, 1,000 write/delete operations, and 1 GB storage per month.
caveats: Performance may vary with high write volumes.
Eventual consistency model.
billingRiskNotes: Usage above free limits incurs charges.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Cloudflare account.
Create a new Worker in the Cloudflare dashboard.
Enable Workers KV and create a namespace.
Bind the KV namespace to your Worker script.
Deploy your Worker and interact with KV using the API.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need low-latency, globally distributed key-value storage for edge applications or serverless workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for high-write, strongly consistent, or relational data needs, or if you require complex querying capabilities.
entry.body: Cloudflare Workers KV provides globally distributed, eventually consistent key-value storage designed for edge applications, offering a generous free tier for low-traffic use cases.

entry.title: Compute Engine e2-micro
entry.description: Google Cloud's Compute Engine e2-micro provides a small, always-free virtual machine for lightweight workloads and testing.

freeTierDetails.summary: 1 e2-micro VM instance per month in select regions, with 30 GB HDD and 1 GB outbound traffic.
caveats: Only available in select US regions
Performance is limited for heavier workloads
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if limits are exceeded or non-free resources are used

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Google Cloud account and enable billing.
Navigate to Compute Engine in the Google Cloud Console.
Create a new VM instance and select the e2-micro machine type.
Choose a supported US region and configure your OS image.
Review and launch the instance, then connect via SSH.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a free, low-resource VM for development, testing, or learning in US regions.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high performance, global regions, or plan to run production workloads with significant resource needs.
entry.body: Compute Engine e2-micro offers a free, entry-level VM ideal for learning, prototyping, and lightweight workloads, with strict regional and resource limits.

entry.title: EMQX Serverless
entry.description: EMQX Serverless provides a fully managed MQTT broker for real-time messaging without infrastructure management.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1 million messages per month and 100 concurrent connections.
caveats: Overages incur charges
Limited to shared infrastructure
billingRiskNotes: Usage beyond free limits is billed automatically

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an EMQX Cloud account.
Navigate to the Serverless MQTT section.
Create a new serverless deployment.
Configure MQTT authentication and topics.
Connect your client devices using the provided endpoint.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed MQTT broker for prototyping, small-scale IoT, or real-time messaging without server management.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require dedicated infrastructure, advanced customizations, or expect high message throughput beyond free limits.
entry.body: EMQX Serverless offers a managed MQTT broker with a generous free tier, ideal for IoT and real-time messaging projects without infrastructure overhead.

entry.title: Firebase Cloud Functions
entry.description: Firebase Cloud Functions lets you run backend code in response to events triggered by Firebase features and HTTPS requests.

freeTierDetails.summary: Generous always-free tier with monthly limits on invocations, compute time, and outbound networking.
caveats: Overages billed automatically
Limits shared across all Firebase projects in the same billing account
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free tier results in charges; monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs.

useCases: Trigger backend logic on Firestore/Realtime Database changes
quickstartSteps: Install Firebase CLI and initialize your project with 'firebase init functions'.
Write your function code in 'functions/index.js' or 'functions/src/index.ts'.
Test functions locally using 'firebase emulators:start'.
Deploy your functions with 'firebase deploy --only functions'.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to run backend code in response to Firebase events or lightweight HTTPS requests without managing servers.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need long-running processes, high compute workloads, or strict cost controls beyond the free tier limits.
entry.body: Firebase Cloud Functions enables you to run serverless backend code triggered by Firebase events or HTTPS requests, ideal for mobile and web app backends.

entry.title: Google Kubernetes Engine
entry.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) provides managed Kubernetes clusters for deploying, managing, and scaling containerized applications on Google Cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: One zonal GKE cluster per month with no cluster management fee.
limits: Compute, storage, and network resources billed separately
caveats: Node VM, storage, and network usage incur standard charges
Free tier applies only to cluster management fee
billingRiskNotes: You pay for VM instances and resources even if cluster management is free

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Google Cloud account and enable billing.
Enable the Kubernetes Engine API in your project.
Install and configure the Google Cloud CLI (gcloud).
Create a zonal GKE cluster using the gcloud CLI or Cloud Console.
Deploy your containerized application to the cluster.
entry.whenToUse: Use GKE when you need a managed Kubernetes environment for scalable container workloads with minimal cluster management overhead.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you want to avoid compute costs entirely or need a fully free, local Kubernetes solution for learning.
entry.body: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) offers a fully managed Kubernetes service, simplifying cluster operations and scaling for containerized applications on Google Cloud.

entry.title: Khan Academy
entry.description: Khan Academy offers free interactive courses and exercises in computer programming, including JavaScript, HTML/CSS, and SQL.

freeTierDetails.summary: All programming courses and exercises are free with no time limits or payment required.
limits: Free access to all programming content
caveats: No official certifications
No private project hosting

quickstartSteps: Visit the Khan Academy programming section.
Sign up for a free account or log in.
Choose a programming course (e.g., JavaScript, HTML/CSS, SQL).
Start the first lesson and complete interactive exercises.
entry.whenToUse: Use Khan Academy to learn programming fundamentals interactively at your own pace, especially if you are new to coding.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced topics, official certifications, or private project hosting for professional development.
entry.body: Khan Academy provides free, interactive programming courses ideal for beginners, covering JavaScript, HTML/CSS, and SQL with hands-on exercises.

entry.title: Lambda
entry.description: AWS Lambda lets you run code in response to events without provisioning or managing servers.

freeTierDetails.summary: 1M free requests and 400,000 GB-seconds compute time per month.
caveats: Limits apply across all Lambda functions in your account.
Additional usage is billed at standard rates.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free tier incurs charges automatically.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an AWS account and log in to the AWS Console.
Navigate to AWS Lambda and click 'Create function'.
Choose 'Author from scratch', enter a function name, and select a runtime.
Write or upload your code, then configure a trigger (e.g., API Gateway or S3 event).
Deploy the function and test it using the AWS Console or CLI.
entry.whenToUse: Use Lambda for running short-lived functions in response to events without managing servers or infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Lambda for long-running tasks, heavy compute workloads, or when you need predictable, sustained compute resources.
entry.body: AWS Lambda is a serverless compute service that runs your code in response to events and automatically manages the underlying compute resources.

entry.title: Oracle Always Free Compute
entry.description: Oracle Always Free Compute offers virtual machines and ARM-based instances with persistent storage at no cost for small workloads.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always Free tier provides up to 4 ARM CPUs, 24 GB RAM, 2 VMs, and 200 GB block storage.
caveats: Limited to specific shapes and regions
Inactive resources may be reclaimed

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Oracle Cloud Free Tier account.
Verify your identity and add a payment method.
Navigate to Compute > Instances in the Oracle Cloud Console.
Create a new instance using an Always Free eligible shape.
Configure networking and storage as needed.
Launch and connect to your VM.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need persistent, low-cost VMs for learning, prototyping, or small production workloads with predictable resource needs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high availability, advanced networking, or need to scale beyond the free tier's strict limits.
entry.body: Oracle Always Free Compute provides a reliable way to run small, persistent VMs at no cost, ideal for learning, prototyping, and open source projects. The free tier includes both ARM and AMD shapes, with generous RAM and storage for entry-level needs. While suitable for light production use, it is not intended for high-availability or large-scale deployments. Resources are capped and may be reclaimed if inactive, so monitor usage to avoid disruption.

entry.title: Supabase Edge Functions
entry.description: Supabase Edge Functions lets you deploy TypeScript/JavaScript serverless functions close to your users for low-latency APIs.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 500,000 function invocations per month.
caveats: Cold starts may affect latency
Limited to supported runtimes

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Supabase account.
Create a new project in the Supabase dashboard.
Install the Supabase CLI and initialize your project.
Write your function in the 'functions' directory.
Deploy your function using the Supabase CLI.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to deploy lightweight serverless APIs or webhooks close to your users with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require custom runtimes, long-running processes, or advanced networking features not supported by edge functions.
entry.body: Supabase Edge Functions provides a simple way to deploy serverless TypeScript/JavaScript functions at the edge, ideal for APIs and webhooks with generous free usage.

entry.title: Virtual Machines
entry.description: Azure Virtual Machines provide scalable, on-demand compute resources for running applications and workloads in the cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: 750 hours per month of B1S Windows or Linux VM for 12 months.
caveats: After 12 months, standard pay-as-you-go rates apply.
Storage and bandwidth beyond free limits incur charges.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free hours or storage incurs charges.
VMs left running beyond free quota will be billed.

useCases: Learning Linux or Windows server administration
quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Azure account and activate the free tier.
Navigate to the Azure Portal and select 'Create a resource'.
Choose 'Virtual Machine', select B1S size, and configure OS and settings.
Review and create the VM, then connect using SSH or RDP.
Monitor usage to stay within free tier limits.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need flexible, on-demand compute for development, testing, or learning with minimal upfront cost.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require always-free compute, need high-performance VMs, or want to avoid potential overage charges after 12 months.
entry.body: Azure Virtual Machines offer a flexible way to run Linux or Windows workloads in the cloud, ideal for learning, prototyping, and testing with a generous 12-month free tier.
